steps.title: Look for different gap sizes
steps.content: The statement says spoilers increased enjoyment for every story, but the size of that increase varied. The best pair must show noticeably different spoiled-versus-unspoiled gaps, not merely two stories with similar effects.

steps.title: Reject similar comparisons
steps.content: Choice A and Choice D pair stories that do not best show a strong contrast in gap size. Choice C pairs stories that do not illustrate the clearest variation because their spoiler-effect gaps are not the most contrasting pair.

steps.title: Choose the clearest contrast
steps.content: Choice B pairs Owl Creek Bridge and A Chess Problem, which show contrasting gap sizes between spoiled and unspoiled enjoyment ratings. That best illustrates how the degree of the spoiler effect varied. The answer is B.
